meaning: adj.暗的,朦胧的,模糊的,晦涩的vt.使暗,使不明显
examples: Her poetry is full of obscure literary allusion.
We mustn't let these minor details obscure the main issue.
I found her lecture very obscure.
And the real estate market takeoff can obscure what is still a thicket of arcane regulations on development.
At an obscure Russian startup, Alexander Yakunin, a theoretical physicist by training, is writing brilliant code.
